pi_items.source: Task description (~/attic_temp_backup.csv) and export_result.sh line 10. The ~ expands to /home/ga based on environment configuration (env.json specifies user 'ga')
setup_task.sh line 24: curl parameter 'name=attic_temp' and task description references 'attic_temp' feed throughout
setup_task.sh line 72: curl command posts to '/input/post?...&json={temp:20}' which creates input named 'temp'
setup_task.sh line 72: curl parameter '&node=attic' and task description references 'attic:temp' input
Task description: '10-minute (600s) interval' explicitly stated. 10 minutes = 600 seconds confirmed by time conversion
setup_task.sh line 24: feed creation with URL-encoded options parameter '%7B%22interval%22%3A10%7D' which decodes to {"interval":10}

privileged_info_summary: The task migrates an Emoncms feed named 'attic_temp' from a 10-second logging interval to a 600-second (10-minute) interval using the PHPFina engine. The feed is associated with the 'attic:temp' input. The initial data is synthetic, generated programmatically with 24 hours of simulated temperature values using a sine wave pattern with random noise, inserted via Emoncms's /feed/insert.json API. The task requires exporting existing data to /home/ga/attic_temp_backup.csv, deleting the old feed, creating a new feed with the correct interval, and updating the input's process list to log to the new feed (process ID 1).
